arguments.file_text:
# HelpChatbot — Implementation Plan

## Problem
Build a web-based chatbot using Blazor Server that answers customer questions about internal product PDF documentation. The chatbot uses an LLM via a RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) pipeline: PDFs are parsed, chunked, embedded, and stored in Postgres with pgvector. User queries are embedded, matched against stored chunks, and the most relevant context is sent to the LLM to generate an answer with source attribution.

## Technology Decisions

| Concern | Choice | Rationale |
|---------|--------|-----------|
| Web UI | Blazor Server (Interactive) | Real-time streaming of LLM responses via SignalR; no separate API layer needed |
| LLM abstraction | Microsoft.Extensions.AI (`IChatClient`, `IEmbeddingGenerator`) | Provider-agnostic, DI-friendly, supports streaming |
| LLM provider | OpenAI (via `OpenAI` NuGet) | Wired into MEAI via `AddChatClient` / `AddEmbeddingGenerator` |
| Vector storage | Postgres + pgvector | Customer requirement; stores embeddings alongside structured data |
| Structured data | EF Core + Npgsql | Chat sessions, messages, and document chunks in Postgres |
| PDF extraction | UglyToad.PdfPig | Pure .NET, Apache 2.0, reliable text extraction |
| Embedding model | `text-embedding-3-small` | Cost-effective, 1536-dim, good quality for RAG |
| Chat model | `gpt-4o-mini` (pinned version) | Cost-effective for Q&A; temperature=0 for factual responses |

## Architecture

```
User ↔ Blazor Server UI (Chat.razor)
             ↕
        ChatService (RAG orchestration)
         ↙         ↘
VectorSearchService   IChatClient (MEAI → OpenAI)
         ↓
   Postgres + pgvector
         ↑
DocumentIngestionService (startup/on-demand)
         ↑
    PDF files in /Docs
```

## Project Structure

```
HelpChatbot/
├── Program.cs                          # DI registration, middleware, DB init
├── appsettings.json                    # Config (connection string, AI settings)
├── Components/
│   ├── _Imports.razor
│   ├── App.razor
│   ├── Routes.razor
│   ├── Layout/
│   │   ├── MainLayout.razor
│   │   └── MainLayout.razor.css
│   └── Pages/
│       ├── Home.razor
│       └── Chat.razor                  # Main chat page with streaming
├── Models/
│   ├── ChatSession.cs                  # Chat session entity
│   ├── ChatMessage.cs                  # Chat message entity (user + assistant)
│   └── DocumentChunk.cs               # Document chunk with embedding vector
├── Data/
│   └── ChatDbContext.cs                # EF Core DbContext
├── Services/
│   ├── DocumentIngestionService.cs     # PDF parse → chunk → embed → store
│   ├── VectorSearchService.cs          # pgvector similarity search
│   └── ChatService.cs                  # RAG pipeline: query → search → prompt → respond
├── Docs/
│   └── product-guide.pdf              # (existing) product documentation
└── wwwroot/
    └── css/
        └── app.css                     # Minimal chat styling
```

## Implementation Todos

### 1. Add NuGet packages
Install all required packages:
- `Microsoft.Extensions.AI` (MEAI abstractions)
- `OpenAI` (LLM provider)
- `Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.Web` (Blazor)
- `Npgsql.EntityFrameworkCore.PostgreSQL` (EF Core for Postgres)
- `Npgsql` (pgvector support via raw SQL for vector ops)
- `UglyToad.PdfPig` (PDF text extraction)

### 2. Create data models

The chatbot uses an LLM via a RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) pipeline: PDFs are parsed, chunked, embedded, and stored in Postgres with pgvector. User queries are embedded, matched against stored chunks, and the most relevant context is sent to the LLM to generate an answer with source attribution.

| Concern | Choice | Rationale |

| LLM provider | OpenAI (via `OpenAI` NuGet) | Wired into MEAI via `AddChatClient` / `AddEmbeddingGenerator` |
| Vector storage | Postgres + pgvector | Customer requirement; stores embeddings alongside structured data |
| Structured data | EF Core + Npgsql | Chat sessions, messages, and document chunks in Postgres |
| PDF extraction | UglyToad.PdfPig | Pure .NET, Apache 2.0, reliable text extraction |
| Embedding model | `text-embedding-3-small` | Cost-effective, 1536-dim, good quality for RAG |
| Chat model | `gpt-4o-mini` (pinned version) | Cost-effective for Q&A; temperature=0 for factual responses |

```
User ↔ Blazor Server UI (Chat.razor)

         ↙         ↘

   Postgres + pgvector
```

- Create HNSW index on embedding column for fast similarity search

- Extract text per page using PdfPig
- Chunk text by paragraphs (~500 tokens per chunk, with overlap)
- Generate embeddings via `IEmbeddingGenerator<string, Embedding<float>>`
- Batch-upsert chunks into Postgres (skip if already ingested, keyed by filename + content hash)
- Run at startup via `IHostedService`

- Accept a query string, generate its embedding
- Execute pgvector cosine similarity search (`<=>` operator)
- Return top-5 chunks with similarity score ≥ 0.70

- Call VectorSearchService to retrieve relevant chunks

- Persist user message + assistant response to ChatMessage table
- Return response with source references

- Register `IEmbeddingGenerator` for embeddings
- Register EF Core DbContext with Postgres connection string
- Register application services (ChatService, VectorSearchService, DocumentIngestionService)
- Set temperature=0, pin model version, configure retry logic
- Add Blazor Server middleware
- Load API key from configuration (env var / user-secrets)

### 8. Build Blazor UI
- `App.razor`, `Routes.razor`, `MainLayout.razor` — standard Blazor scaffolding
- `Chat.razor` — main chat page:
  - Text input + send button
  - Message list (user messages right-aligned, assistant left-aligned)
  - Streaming display of assistant responses
  - Source attribution links below assistant messages
  - New session button
- `app.css` — clean, minimal chat styling

### 9. Add configuration
- `appsettings.json` with placeholders for:
  - `ConnectionStrings:ChatDb` (Postgres connection string)
  - `AI:ApiKey` (OpenAI API key — NOT committed, use user-secrets)
  - `AI:ChatModel` (pinned model version)
  - `AI:EmbeddingModel` (embedding model name)
  - `AI:DocsPath` (path to documentation folder)

### 10. Validate
- `dotnet build` compiles cleanly
- Verify project structure is complete and wired up
- Confirm all DI registrations resolve correctly

## Guardrails Applied (from technology-selection guidance)
- ✅ MEAI abstraction layer — no direct OpenAI SDK calls in business logic
- ✅ Temperature = 0 for factual responses
- ✅ Model version pinned (gpt-4o-mini dated version)
- ✅ API key from secure config (user-secrets), not hardcoded
- ✅ Retry logic on LLM calls
- ✅ Streaming responses for UX
- ✅ RAG with relevance threshold (0.70 minimum similarity)
- ✅ Source attribution on every answer
- ✅ Batch embeddings during ingestion
- ✅ Skip re-embedding already-ingested content

## Notes
- The pgvector extension must be installed on the Postgres instance (`CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS vector`)
- For production: add authentication, rate limiting, and token budget tracking
- The `Docs/` folder is scanned at startup; drop new PDFs there and restart to ingest
- Chat history is persisted per session for multi-turn conversations

# HelpChatbot — Implementation Plan

## Problem
Build a web-based chatbot using Blazor Server that answers customer questions about internal product PDF documentation. The chatbot uses an LLM via a RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) pipeline: PDFs are parsed, chunked, embedded, and stored in Postgres with pgvector. User queries are embedded, matched against stored chunks, and the most relevant context is sent to the LLM to generate an answer with source attribution.

| Concern | Choice | Rationale |
|---------|--------|-----------|
| Web UI | Blazor Server (Interactive) | Real-time streaming of LLM responses via SignalR; no separate API layer needed |
| LLM abstraction | Microsoft.Extensions.AI (`IChatClient`, `IEmbeddingGenerator`) | Provider-agnostic, DI-friendly, supports streaming |
| LLM provider | OpenAI (via `OpenAI` NuGet) | Wired into MEAI via `AddChatClient` / `AddEmbeddingGenerator` |
| Vector storage | Postgres + pgvector | Customer requirement; stores embeddings alongside structured data |
| Structured data | EF Core + Npgsql | Chat sessions, messages, and document chunks in Postgres |
| PDF extraction | UglyToad.PdfPig | Pure .NET, Apache 2.0, reliable text extraction |
| Embedding model | `text-embedding-3-small` | Cost-effective, 1536-dim, good quality for RAG |
| Chat model | `gpt-4o-mini` (pinned version) | Cost-effective for Q&A; temperature=0 for factual responses |

### 1. Add NuGet packages
Install all required packages:
- `Microsoft.Extensions.AI` (MEAI abstractions)
- `OpenAI` (LLM provider)
- `Npgsql.EntityFrameworkCore.PostgreSQL` (EF Core for Postgres)
- `Npgsql` (pgvector support via raw SQL for vector ops)
- `UglyToad.PdfPig` (PDF text extraction)

### 2. Create data models
- `DocumentChunk`: Id, FileName, PageNumber, ChunkIndex, Content, Embedding (float[]), ContentHash, CreatedAt
- `ChatSession`: Id, Title, CreatedAt
- `ChatMessage`: Id, SessionId, Role (user/assistant), Content, Sources (JSON), CreatedAt

- `ChatDbContext` with DbSets for all three entities
- Configure pgvector column type for embeddings
- Ensure pgvector extension is created on DB initialization
- Create HNSW index on embedding column for fast similarity search

- Scan `Docs/` folder for PDF files
- Extract text per page using PdfPig
- Chunk text by paragraphs (~500 tokens per chunk, with overlap)
- Generate embeddings via `IEmbeddingGenerator<string, Embedding<float>>`
- Batch-upsert chunks into Postgres (skip if already ingested via content hash)
- Run at startup via `IHostedService`

- Accept a query string, generate its embedding
- Execute pgvector cosine similarity search (`<=>` operator)
- Return top-5 chunks with similarity score ≥ 0.70
- Include source attribution (filename, page number)

- Accept user question + session ID
- Call VectorSearchService to retrieve relevant chunks
- Construct system prompt with retrieved context and instructions
- Call `IChatClient.GetStreamingResponseAsync()` for streamed response
- Persist user message + assistant response to ChatMessage table
- Return response with source references

### 7. Configure DI and middleware in Program.cs
- Register `IChatClient` via `AddChatClient` with OpenAI provider
- Register `IEmbeddingGenerator` for embeddings
- Register EF Core DbContext with Postgres connection string
- Register application services
- Set temperature=0, pin model version, configure retry logic
- Add Blazor Server middleware
- Load API key from configuration (env var / user-secrets)

- `App.razor`, `Routes.razor`, `MainLayout.razor` — standard Blazor scaffolding
- `Chat.razor` — main chat page:
  - Text input + send button
  - Message list (user/assistant bubbles)
  - Streaming display of assistant responses
  - Source attribution below assistant messages
  - New session button
- `app.css` — clean, minimal chat styling

### 9. Add configuration
- `appsettings.json` with placeholders for:
  - `ConnectionStrings:ChatDb` (Postgres)
  - `AI:ApiKey` (OpenAI — use user-secrets, NOT committed)
  - `AI:ChatModel` / `AI:EmbeddingModel`
  - `AI:DocsPath` (documentation folder path)

### 10. Validate
- `dotnet build` compiles cleanly
- Verify project structure is complete and wired correctly

## Guardrails Applied
- ✅ MEAI abstraction layer — no direct OpenAI SDK calls in business logic
- ✅ Temperature = 0 for factual responses
- ✅ Model version pinned
- ✅ API key from secure config (user-secrets), not hardcoded
- ✅ Retry logic on LLM calls
- ✅ Streaming responses for UX
- ✅ RAG with relevance threshold (0.70 minimum similarity)
- ✅ Source attribution on every answer
- ✅ Batch embeddings during ingestion
- ✅ Skip re-embedding already-ingested content

## Notes
- The pgvector extension must be installed on the Postgres instance
- For production: add authentication, rate limiting, and token budget tracking
- Drop new PDFs in `Docs/` and restart to ingest them
- Chat history is persisted per session for multi-turn conversations

- `ChatSession`: Id, Title, CreatedAt
- `ChatMessage`: Id, SessionId, Role (user/assistant), Content, Sources (JSON), CreatedAt

- `ChatDbContext` with DbSets for all three entities
- Configure pgvector column type for embeddings
- Ensure pgvector extension is created on DB initialization
- Create HNSW index on embedding column for fast similarity search

- Scan `Docs/` folder for PDF files

- Chunk text by paragraphs (~500 tokens per chunk, with overlap)
- Generate embeddings via `IEmbeddingGenerator<string, Embedding<float>>`

- Run at startup via `IHostedService`

- Accept a query string, generate its embedding

- Return top-5 chunks with similarity score ≥ 0.70
- Include source attribution (filename, page number)

- Return response with source references

- Set temperature=0, pin model version, configure retry logic
- Add Blazor Server middleware
- Load API key from configuration (env var / user-secrets)

- `App.razor`, `Routes.razor`, `MainLayout.razor` — standard Blazor scaffolding
- `Chat.razor` — main chat page:
  - Text input + send button
  - Message list (user/assistant bubbles)
  - Streaming display of assistant responses
  - Source attribution below assistant messages
  - New session button
- `app.css` — clean, minimal chat styling

### 9. Add configuration
- `appsettings.json` with placeholders for:
  - `ConnectionStrings:ChatDb` (Postgres)
  - `AI:ApiKey` (OpenAI — use user-secrets, NOT committed)
  - `AI:ChatModel` / `AI:EmbeddingModel`
  - `AI:DocsPath` (documentation folder path)

### 10. Validate
- `dotnet build` compiles cleanly
- Verify project structure is complete and wired correctly

## Guardrails Applied
- MEAI abstraction layer — no direct OpenAI SDK calls in business logic
- Temperature = 0 for factual responses
- Model version pinned
- API key from secure config (user-secrets), not hardcoded
- Retry logic on LLM calls
- Streaming responses for UX
- RAG with relevance threshold (0.70 minimum similarity)
- Source attribution on every answer
- Batch embeddings during ingestion
- Skip re-embedding already-ingested content

- The pgvector extension must be installed on the Postgres instance
- For production: add authentication, rate limiting, and token budget tracking

+| Chat model | `gpt-4o-mini` (pinned version) | Cost-effective for Q&A; temperature=0 for factual responses |
+
+## Architecture
+
+```
+User ↔ Blazor Server UI (Chat.razor)
+             ↕
+        ChatService (RAG orchestration)
+         ↙         ↘
+VectorSearchService   IChatClient (MEAI → OpenAI)
+         ↓
+   Postgres + pgvector
+         ↑
+DocumentIngestionService (startup/on-demand)
+         ↑
+    PDF files in /Docs
+```
+
+## Project Structure
+
+```
+HelpChatbot/
+├── Program.cs                          # DI registration, middleware, DB init
+├── appsettings.json                    # Config (connection string, AI settings)
+├── Components/
+│   ├── _Imports.razor
+│   ├── App.razor
+│   ├── Routes.razor
+│   ├── Layout/
+│   │   ├── MainLayout.razor
+│   │   └── MainLayout.razor.css
+│   └── Pages/
+│       ├── Home.razor
+│       └── Chat.razor                  # Main chat page with streaming
+├── Models/
+│   ├── ChatSession.cs                  # Chat session entity
+│   ├── ChatMessage.cs                  # Chat message entity (user + assistant)
+│   └── DocumentChunk.cs               # Document chunk with embedding vector
+├── Data/
+│   └── ChatDbContext.cs                # EF Core DbContext
+├── Services/
+│   ├── DocumentIngestionService.cs     # PDF parse → chunk → embed → store
+│   ├── VectorSearchService.cs          # pgvector similarity search
+│   └── ChatService.cs                  # RAG pipeline: query → search → prompt → respond
+├── Docs/
+│   └── product-guide.pdf              # (existing) product documentation
+└── wwwroot/
+    └── css/
+        └── app.css                     # Minimal chat styling
+```
+
+## Implementation Todos
+
+### 1. Add NuGet packages
+Install all required packages:
+- `Microsoft.Extensions.AI` (MEAI abstractions)
+- `OpenAI` (LLM provider)
+- `Npgsql.EntityFrameworkCore.PostgreSQL` (EF Core for Postgres)
+- `Npgsql` (pgvector support via raw SQL for vector ops)
+- `UglyToad.PdfPig` (PDF text extraction)
+
+### 2. Create data models
+- `DocumentChunk`: Id, FileName, PageNumber, ChunkIndex, Content, Embedding (float[]), ContentHash, CreatedAt
+- `ChatSession`: Id, Title, CreatedAt
+- `ChatMessage`: Id, SessionId, Role (user/assistant), Content, Sources (JSON), CreatedAt
+
+### 3. Create EF Core DbContext
+- `ChatDbContext` with DbSets for all three entities
+- Configure pgvector column type for embeddings
+- Ensure pgvector extension is created on DB initialization
+- Create HNSW index on embedding column for fast similarity search
+
+### 4. Build DocumentIngestionService
+- Scan `Docs/` folder for PDF files
+- Extract text per page using PdfPig
+- Chunk text by paragraphs (~500 tokens per chunk, with overlap)
+- Generate embeddings via `IEmbeddingGenerator<string, Embedding<float>>`
+- Batch-upsert chunks into Postgres (skip if already ingested via content hash)
+- Run at startup via `IHostedService`
+
+### 5. Build VectorSearchService
+- Accept a query string, generate its embedding
+- Execute pgvector cosine similarity search (`<=>` operator)
+- Return top-5 chunks with similarity score ≥ 0.70
+- Include source attribution (filename, page number)
+
+### 6. Build ChatService (RAG pipeline)
+- Accept user question + session ID
+- Call VectorSearchService to retrieve relevant chunks
+- Construct system prompt with retrieved context and instructions
+- Call `IChatClient.GetStreamingResponseAsync()` for streamed response
+- Persist user message + assistant response to ChatMessage table
+- Return response with source references
+
+### 7. Configure DI and middleware in Program.cs
+- Register `IChatClient` via `AddChatClient` with OpenAI provider
+- Register `IEmbeddingGenerator` for embeddings
+- Register EF Core DbContext with Postgres connection string
+- Register application services
+- Set temperature=0, pin model version, configure retry logic
+- Add Blazor Server middleware
+- Load API key from configuration (env var / user-secrets)
+
+### 8. Build Blazor UI
+- `App.razor`, `Routes.razor`, `MainLayout.razor` — standard Blazor scaffolding
+- `Chat.razor` — main chat page:
+  - Text input + send button
+  - Message list (user/assistant bubbles)
+  - Streaming display of assistant responses
+  - Source attribution below assistant messages
+  - New session button
+- `app.css` — clean, minimal chat styling
+
+### 9. Add configuration
+- `appsettings.json` with placeholders for:
+  - `ConnectionStrings:ChatDb` (Postgres)
+  - `AI:ApiKey` (OpenAI — use user-secrets, NOT committed)
+  - `AI:ChatModel` / `AI:EmbeddingModel`
+  - `AI:DocsPath` (documentation folder path)
 
+### 10. Validate
+- `dotnet build` compiles cleanly
+- Verify project structure is complete and wired correctly
+
+## Guardrails Applied
+- MEAI abstraction layer — no direct OpenAI SDK calls in business logic
+- Temperature = 0 for factual responses
+- Model version pinned
+- API key from secure config (user-secrets), not hardcoded
+- Retry logic on LLM calls
+- Streaming responses for UX
+- RAG with relevance threshold (0.70 minimum similarity)
+- Source attribution on every answer
+- Batch embeddings during ingestion
+- Skip re-embedding already-ingested content
+
+## Notes
+- The pgvector extension must be installed on the Postgres instance
+- For production: add authentication, rate limiting, and token budget tracking
+- Drop new PDFs in `Docs/` and restart to ingest them
+- Chat history is persisted per session for multi-turn conversations

- **Microsoft.Extensions.AI** — provider-agnostic LLM/embedding abstraction (no direct OpenAI SDK calls in business logic)

- **Postgres + pgvector** — embeddings and structured data in one database
- **UglyToad.PdfPig** — PDF text extraction
